picks.reasoning: Detroit (48-18) is on a 3-game win streak vs. a middling Toronto squad (37-29) with nothing to play for; multiple analysts rated this the clearest spread play of the day.
picks.sources: Bleacher Nation, Pickswise, Dimers (3/3 expert sources agree)
picks.result_notes: Brandon Ingram erupted for 34 points; Pistons lost by 11 on the road despite being consensus favorites.

picks.reasoning: Cleveland blew out Dallas by 33 points two days ago in Dallas with starters under 30 minutes; sharp money returning to Cavs as Dallas travels demoralized.
picks.result_notes: Dallas stunned Cleveland on the road; Cooper Flagg (27 pts) and Naji Marshall (25 pts) led an unexpected upset — revenge game narrative backfired.

picks.reasoning: OKC is elite but 8.5–9.5 pts is too generous; Minnesota won the last meeting 123-111, and their defense prevents blowouts even in hostile environments.
picks.result_notes: Thunder won by 13, blowing past the spread; OKC on an 8-game win streak — Minnesota's defense couldn't contain SGA (20/10) and Holmgren (21/9).

picks.reasoning: OKC controls pace at home; Minnesota's elite defense suppresses scoring; multiple analysts project the game landing in the low-220s.
picks.result_notes: Total of 219 came in well under 226 as predicted — Minnesota's defense held even in a loss.

picks.game: Golden State Warriors @ New York Knicks
picks.reasoning: KAT shooting 37% from deep at home (1.6 made/game at MSG); Warriors allowing a league-worst 13 made threes per game over their last 10 contests — optimal matchup for a multi-three game.
picks.sources: Bleacher Nation, RotoWire, ESPN Fantasy, Basketball.org.nz (5+ sources)
picks.result_notes: KAT went 0-for-2 from three despite a favorable matchup; focused on interior scoring (17 pts on 7-12 FG) as Knicks rallied from 21 down to win 110-107.

picks.reasoning: Dallas got torched by 33 at home vs. Cleveland two days ago with Cavs starters playing under 30 minutes; traveling to Cleveland again in a demoralized state with no elite scorer to lean on.
picks.result_notes: Dallas flipped the script completely — Flagg and Marshall combined for 52 points; 'demoralized' narrative was wrong, Dallas came out motivated.

picks.reasoning: Dimers model: 57.6% probability, 5.2% edge — strongest model play on the full 8-game Monday slate. 243.5 is the highest total of the day for a non-marquee matchup; pace/defense analysis suppresses combined scoring. Our only profitable edge type (1-0 on totals).
picks.sources: Dimers simulation model, BettingPros, multiple best-bet aggregators
picks.result_notes: Total of 239 came in well under 243.5 — Bulls blew out Memphis by 25; blowout game script suppressed garbage-time offense and kept combined scoring low.

picks.game: Oklahoma City Thunder vs. Orlando Magic
picks.reasoning: Orlando on B2B (lost to ATL 112-124 last night) + 3 key players OUT (Wagner, Isaac, Black) + OKC has league-best defense (107.72 DRtg #1 in NBA). Depleted, fatigued Magic cannot score vs elite OKC defense at home.
picks.sources: Basketball-Reference ratings, ESPN injury report, ESPN schedule (B2B confirmed)
picks.result_notes: Total of 221 came in under 223.5 — depleted Orlando (B2B, 3 players OUT) held to 108 by OKC's elite defense despite Banchero's 32 pts.

picks.reasoning: Phoenix on B2B road trip (lost to BOS 112-120 last night). Rested Minnesota at home (41-27, 6th West). PHX fatigue suppresses offense in 2nd road game in 2 nights. MIN defense held OKC game to 219 combined (3/15).
picks.sources: ESPN schedule, Basketball-Reference team records, ESPN injury report
picks.result_notes: Total of 220 came in well under 228.5 — Phoenix's B2B fatigue showed as Suns scored only 104; Randle (32 pts) led Minnesota's controlled home win.

picks.reasoning: Brooklyn has failed to score 100 points in 5 consecutive games — one of the worst offensive stretches in the league. Even at Sacramento's normal pace (~115 pts), Brooklyn's ceiling at ~95-100 puts combined total at ~210-215, well inside 218.5.

Phoenix on B2B (played 3/21), 5-game losing streak, Brooks OUT, Williams OUT, Allen QUESTIONABLE. Line moved 3.5 pts (Suns -1.5 → Raptors -2) — largest swing on Sunday board, clear sharp money signal. Toronto rested with 20-14 ATS road record.
picks.sources: Action Network line movement, Pickswise best bet, ESPN injury report (B2B confirmed)

units.note: Assuming 1u per pick. Unders at -110 pay 0.91u each (x4 wins = 3.64u). All losses = -1u each.

_note_mar16: Mar 16 2026: 1 pick only (POTD). Rejected 6 other potential picks per lessons.md rules. Discipline over quantity.
_note_mar17: Mar 17 2026: 2 picks only (POTD + Runner-Up). Both unders driven by B2B + defense/schedule edge. Rejected 5 games per lessons.md rules. All picks are totals (our 2-0 edge type).
_note_mar22: Mar 22 2026: 2 picks only (POTD + Runner-Up). POTD is a totals under on Nets @ Kings (Brooklyn 0-100 in 5 straight). Runner-up is Raptors -2 vs B2B Suns with 3.5-pt sharp line movement. No props (0-1 record + game-script risk). No fade (no data-backed candidate).

pick_entry.edge_type: rest | pace | defense | sharp_money | total | schedule | prop_stat | consensus (what kind of edge drove this pick)
pick_entry.units: Number of units risked (default 1, use 0.5 for low confidence, 2 for rare high-conviction plays)
pick_entry.reasoning: Why this pick was made — MUST reference specific stats, not just 'experts agree'
pick_entry.result: W | L | P | CANCELLED | null (null = unsettled)
pick_entry.actual_score: Final score or stat line (e.g. 'DEN 112 - LAL 108' or 'KAT: 0-2 3PT')
pick_entry.result_notes: Why the pick won/lost (1 sentence post-game analysis)
